title: Creating a Cutting Tool (Flint Knapping)
symptoms: Have glass, flint, obsidian, or quartz
immediate_steps: Find a brittle stone like flint, chert, obsidian, or use the thick glass bottom of a broken bottle
Use a harder, round 'hammerstone' to strike the edge of the material at a downward angle
Use the flakes directly for cutting meat, cordage, or carving wood
Wrap the dull side of the flake in leather or cloth to protect your hand
avoid: Do not strike without eye protection if possible (squint or look away upon impact)
